$comment: Harness scope surface (note 9). The foundational data structure: scheduler picks the in_progress item, verifier gates active->passing, handoff reads state. JSON (not MD) so it isn't casually overwritten. Every feature starts failing; it must EARN 'passing' via its verification command + recorded evidence (commit hash). Hooks: check-wip.py (exactly one in_progress), check-evidence.py (no 'passing' without evidence).
rules: One feature in_progress at a time. active->passing ONLY when `verification` runs green AND `evidence` records the proof (commit hash + verify reference). Do not hand-edit a feature to 'passing'.

features.behavior: Reconcile stale feature_list.json statuses + refresh PROGRESS.md: confirm R2.2x tests pass, fix -k selectors to match real test names, update PROGRESS.md to reflect true HEAD (core/infra done; gateway done; worker idle skeleton; next = Phase 1 domain cards). Fix Ryuk testcontainers issue so integration tests run cleanly.
verification: python .claude/scripts/check-wip.py && python .claude/scripts/check-evidence.py (exit 0)
uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'schemas or error_handlers' (10 passed)
uv run pytest tests/integration -k ingestion (7 passed)
features.evidence: 49 unit + 7 integration passed (make check green); Ryuk fix in conftest.py; tracker reconciled; commit ed6693e HEAD

features.behavior: Vendor simulation & fault injection (the probe substrate per SPEC 1-2): simulate parse/TTS vendor calls with asyncio.sleep + seedable randomized failure. Parse injects a 15% 500-error rate (transient -> exercises retry). A deterministic 'poison' manuscript always fails (-> exercises DLQ after 3). Seedable so failure behavior is unit-assertable.
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k fault_injection (rate=0.0 never fails; rate=1.0 always fails; fixed seed reproducible; poison manuscript always raises SAME retryable VendorError; parse returns D3 blocks; tts keyed on D4 hash; pure, no Docker)

features.behavior: Pure domain splitter (BOM 02-D3, feeds R2.3): split_blocks(manuscript) in core/domain/text.py splits a manuscript on blank-line boundaries (paragraph = block), strips whitespace, drops empties. Empty/whitespace-only -> [] (0-block path). One paragraph -> [that]. Deterministic; the fan-out width comes from here. MUST NOT raise on empty/huge input (bounding is W3/H14's job); MUST stay I/O-free (architecture test).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k text (empty->[], whitespace->[], 1 para->1, 3 paras->3, leading/trailing blanks ignored)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k text -> 16 passed (0/1/3-block, strip, blank-line collapse, whitespace-only separator, soft-newline kept, deterministic, parametrized fan-out width); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 41 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 68 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Pure canonical content hasher (BOM 02-D4, feeds R4.2 cache + R2.3 Task.block_hash): content_hash(text) in core/domain/hash.py returns sha256(text.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest() — a 64-char hex digest, stable across processes/runs (no salt). MUST hash the block TEXT, never the task_id (conflating the vendor-call cache key with the fan-in counter key is the named junior trap). Single canonical hasher reused by W3 (Task.block_hash) and W4 (cache + tts/<hash>.wav key). MUST stay I/O-free (architecture test).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k hash (same text->same hash; different text->different; 64-char lowercase hex; known vectors for fixed strings)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k hash -> 12 passed (known vectors for '' and 'hello world', determinism, distinctness, 64-char lowercase hex, whitespace-significant, utf-8 non-ASCII, parametrized idempotency); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 43 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 80 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Compare-and-set transition contract (BOM 02-D2, hardens R1.2 state.py): document in state.py that status writes MUST go through CAS (UPDATE jobs SET status=:next WHERE job_id=:id AND status IN (:expected)) not read-then-write, and that a rowcount-0 CAS is a NORMAL concurrent outcome ('someone else advanced it'), NOT an error — handlers treat it as already-advanced and proceed idempotently. Add pure helper expected_for(next) -> set[JobStatus] returning legal predecessors so handlers build the WHERE guard from one source of truth. MUST stay I/O-free (no SQLAlchemy in domain; the SQL lives in the Phase 3/4 handler).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'state_machine or fsm' (existing transitions still green + expected_for predecessors correct, terminal states have no predecessors, inverse of can_transition)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'state_machine or fsm' -> 12 passed (8 original transition tests still green + 4 new: forward-path predecessors, FAILED=all-non-terminal, PENDING no-predecessors, exact-inverse-of-can_transition property); CAS contract + rowcount-0-is-normal documented in state.py docstring; domain stays I/O-free (test_architecture green); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 43 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 84 passed; commit 0aff00f HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Async Redis client adapter (BOM 06-R1, root of rung R4.1): core/infra/redis.py get_redis(url) returns redis.from_url(url, decode_responses=False) (NOT awaited; keep bytes for hashes/tokens); async ping(client)->bool health helper. Uses redis.asyncio (NOT sync redis, NOT dead aioredis). Build once, share; close via aclose() (8.x rename). Mirrors infra/db.py get_engine shape.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (ping round-trips against a real Redis container)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 2 passed (ping round-trips; decode_responses=False keeps bytes) against a real redis:7-alpine testcontainer; get_redis/ping in core/infra/redis.py replace the docstring stub; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 1 file (whole-project make check) 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Leased N-token Redis semaphore (BOM 06-R2, Constraint A: global TTS limit=3 across ALL workers): Semaphore(slots_key, slots, lease_ttl) with acquire(owner)->token via blocking BLPOP (no busy-poll) + SET lease key NX EX, release(token) deletes lease + RPUSH token back, async-with wrapper releasing even on exception. Never asyncio.Semaphore. Cache checked BEFORE acquire (a hit burns no token).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (slots=2: 3rd acquire blocks until a release; never >slots tokens out)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 6 passed (4 new R2: slots=2 bounds concurrency + 3rd acquire blocks then succeeds after release; acquire records tts:lease:<token> with TTL<=lease_ttl; release clears lease + returns token; async-with slot releases token+lease even on exception) against real redis:7-alpine; Semaphore (BLPOP acquire / lease SET NX EX / delete-lease-then-RPUSH release) in core/infra/redis.py; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Semaphore init idempotency (BOM 12-X4, the 3xN-tokens footgun): ensure_slots() seeds exactly N tokens exactly-once across ALL workers via atomic Lua (init-marker: if unset DEL slots_key + RPUSH N tokens 0..N-1 + set marker; else no-op). Converges to exactly N regardless of how many workers call it concurrently. Never unconditional RPUSH on boot.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (5 concurrent ensure_slots -> LLEN tts:slots == N exactly)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 8 passed (2 new X4: 5 workers race ensure_slots concurrently -> LLEN tts:slots == 3 exactly with token identities ['0','1','2']; ensure_slots is init-once-not-top-up: after consuming a token a 2nd ensure_slots leaves 2 not 3) against real redis:7-alpine; atomic Lua (_ENSURE_SLOTS_LUA: init-marker guard + DEL + RPUSH N + SET marker) registered in Semaphore.__init__; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Lease reaper: heartbeat renew + atomic reclaim (BOM 12-X5, H6): acquire spawns a heartbeat task re-SET lease XX EX every ttl/3 (cancel on release) so a healthy-but-slow holder is never reclaimed; reap() atomically (Lua, owner-checked) RPUSHes expired-but-unreturned tokens back exactly once (concurrent reapers return a token at most once). Documented as soft/best-effort limit, breaches logged.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (stopped-heartbeat holder reclaimed after TTL; heartbeating holder keeps token; two reapers reclaim once)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 11 passed (3 new X5: crashed holder's expired-lease token reclaimed by reap()->1; live heartbeating holder outlives TTL and reap()->0 leaves it; two concurrent reapers reclaim a token sum==1 with no double-return) against real redis:7-alpine; acquire spawns a ttl/3 SET-XX heartbeat (cancelled on release), reap() runs atomic owner-safe _RECLAIM_LUA per token, soft-limit breaches logged; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Content-hash cache (BOM 06-R3, Constraint B cost-idempotency): cache_get(h)->str|None (GET tts:cache:{h}), cache_set(h,url) (SETEX tts:cache:{h} CACHE_TTL_S url). Keyed on canonical content_hash (D4), never task_id. CACHE_TTL_S <= MinIO object lifetime (H-DANGLE). Consulted before slot acquire; not durable truth (rebuildable from MinIO).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (set then get returns url; missing hash -> None; entry expires after TTL)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 15 passed (4 new R3: cold miss->None then set->get returns the MinIO url; miss returns None; key is tts:cache:<content_hash(D4)> with TTL 0<ttl<=3600; entry expires after TTL->None) against real redis:7-alpine; Cache class keys on D4 content_hash (never task_id), cache_set uses SET..EX (SETEX deprecated in redis-py 8); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Cache stampede in-flight lock (BOM backlog-H8): acquire_inflight(h)->bool true if caller won SET tts:inflight:{h} NX EX (it synthesizes); losers wait (bounded) for cache_get then use it -> at most one vendor call per identical-block burst. MUST NOT hold a TTS slot while waiting on the in-flight lock. OR a documented deliberate simplification in module + DOC2.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis (K concurrent acquire_inflight for one hash -> exactly one True; rest observe populated cache)
features.evidence: IMPLEMENTED the in-flight lock (not the simplification). uv run pytest tests/integration -k redis -> 17 passed (2 new H8: 10 concurrent acquire_inflight for one hash -> sum(won)==1 exactly one synthesiser; 5-caller stampede -> exactly one 'synth' (vendor call), the other 4 wait_for_cache and all end with the same url) against real redis:7-alpine; Cache.acquire_inflight (SET tts:inflight:<hash> NX EX) / release_inflight / wait_for_cache (bounded poll), checked WITHOUT a TTS slot to avoid pool deadlock; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Idempotency fast-path + inbox helpers (BOM 06-R4 / 03-B5, exactly-once effect): durable mark_event(session, event_id)->bool via dialect insert(ProcessedEvent).on_conflict_do_nothing() (True first / False duplicate) is the AUTHORITY; Redis seen_once(task_id)->bool SETNX fast-path is non-authoritative optimization (H3). NEVER inbox-skip parse (H2). processed_events retention (H10).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or redis' (mark_event twice -> True then False; seen_once twice -> True then False; retention deletes aged rows)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or redis' -> 24 passed (3 new: db.mark_event (pg dialect insert ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) -> True first / False duplicate as the durable AUTHORITY; db.purge_processed_events deletes rows older than window (H10 retention, DB-side now()) -> deleted==1 aged gone fresh kept; redis.seen_once SETNX fast-path -> True then False, non-authoritative H3) against real postgres:17-alpine + redis:7-alpine; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 44 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit 6203aeb HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Atomic fan-in decrement guarded in-transaction (BOM 03-B4, the highest-weight state-across-boundaries mechanism): complete_task_and_decrement(session, job_id, task_id, audio_key)->int|None in core/infra/queries.py does, in ONE transaction, a durable idempotent claim (UPDATE tasks SET status='DONE', audio_key WHERE task_id AND status<>'DONE') then, ONLY if rowcount==1, an atomic barrier decrement (UPDATE jobs SET pending_count=pending_count-1 RETURNING pending_count). Returns remaining (caller emits StitchReady when 0) or None (already-counted duplicate -> no decrement). H3: the dup-guard is the in-tx conditional UPDATE, NEVER Redis SETNX (evictable -> double-decrement -> early StitchReady -> incomplete drama marked COMPLETED). Exactly one caller observes remaining==0 under concurrent redelivery; never read-subtract-write (lost update).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or fan_in' (N tasks decrement to exactly 0 once; same task delivered twice decrements once; concurrent decrements lose no update)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or fan_in' -> 9 passed (3 new B4 fan_in: N tasks decrement N..0 with exactly one observed 0; SAME task delivered twice -> 2nd claim rowcount 0 -> None, counter decremented exactly once; 8 concurrent decrements via asyncio.gather across SEPARATE sessions -> results == perm(0..7), exactly one 0, final pending_count==0) against real postgres:17-alpine; complete_task_and_decrement in core/infra/queries.py (in-tx conditional tasks.status claim = H3 durable authority + atomic UPDATE jobs SET pending_count=pending_count-1 RETURNING, never read-subtract-write); full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 45 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit b891a95 HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Set pending_count only on the first CAS out of PENDING (BOM backlog-H15, parse re-runnability corollary of H2): begin_parse(session, job_id, n_blocks)->bool does a CAS UPDATE jobs SET status=PARSING, pending_count=N WHERE job_id AND status=PENDING. rowcount==1 => first run (counter set, returns True); rowcount==0 => parse redelivery (job already advanced, counter untouched, returns False). A parse re-run MUST NOT reset pending_count (that would resurrect already-decremented tasks and the job would never finish). NOTE: FSM legal successor of PENDING is PARSING (state.py LEGAL), not GENERATING as the card title loosely states.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or counter_once' (begin_parse twice -> True then False; counter set exactly once; a re-run leaves a decremented counter intact)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'models or counter_once' -> 12 passed (3 new H15 counter_once: first begin_parse CAS PENDING->PARSING seeds pending_count=5 and returns True; after a simulated decrement to 4 a redelivery returns False and leaves the counter at 4 NOT reset to 5; 5 concurrent begin_parse -> exactly one True, counter seeded once at 7) against real postgres:17-alpine; begin_parse in core/infra/queries.py does the FSM-legal PENDING->PARSING CAS (state.py LEGAL forbids PENDING->GENERATING; card title loose) setting pending_count only on rowcount==1; full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 45 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 82 passed; commit b891a95 HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Worker bootstrap / DI wiring (BOM 12-X3): build_context() -> WorkerContext constructs and wires every adapter the handlers need (broker connection+channel+exchange via declare_full, db engine, shared redis client + Semaphore + Cache, MinIO client+ensure_bucket) from get_settings(); calls configure_logging() and Semaphore.ensure_slots() (X4 idempotent seed) exactly once on boot. close_context() tears everything down cleanly. Worker imports core only, never gateway (boundary).

features.behavior: Handler dispatch / DI table (BOM 12-X2): build_handlers(ctx) -> {Q_PARSE: parse_handler(ctx), Q_TTS: tts_handler(ctx), Q_STITCH: stitch_handler(ctx)} maps each queue to its context-injected handler so the consume loop is generic. Each handler parses its event via pydantic model_validate_json (pointers only, never bytes). Adapters injected via ctx, never hardcoded.

features.behavior: Worker entrypoint & run loop (BOM 12-X1): replace the idle await asyncio.Future() in worker/main.py with a real run loop: build_context() (X3) -> register the three consumers (X2 dispatch) with sized prefetch (W1) -> await shutdown. Clean SIGTERM/SIGINT handling closes the broker connection and cancels the run task so unacked messages are released for redelivery (R3.1 crash semantics).

features.behavior: Consume loop with manual-ack + sized prefetch (BOM 08-W1, H-PREFETCH): register handlers via broker.consume with no_ack=False (manual ack LAST); size q.tts prefetch near the semaphore size (slots + small headroom), NOT the global PREFETCH=16 (13+ messages parked unacked on a blocked BLPOP = large crash blast radius + HOL pressure). Do NOT use the ref repo's auto-ack process() loop (H-REF1).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k prefetch (q.tts prefetch sized near TTS_CONCURRENCY, not 16)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k prefetch -> 2 passed (prefetch_for(q.tts) <= TTS_CONCURRENCY+1 and < global PREFETCH=16; q.parse/q.stitch == PREFETCH); prefetch_for in worker/main.py wired into register_consumers (one channel per queue so set_qos is per-queue). The live crash-redelivery blast-radius assertion is Phase-6 R3.1 e2e; here L1+L2. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 52 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 87 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Exception taxonomy (BOM 12-X7): worker/errors.py defines TransientError (retryable -> retry ladder) and PoisonError (non-retryable -> DLQ immediately). Unknown/unclassified exceptions are treated as transient up to MAX_RETRIES then DLQ'd (fail-safe: never silently ack-drop a message because of an unclassified bug).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'errors or taxonomy' (two classes; documented routing: poison->DLQ now, transient/unknown->ladder)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k errors -> 3 passed (TransientError/PoisonError are Exceptions; is_poison(PoisonError)=True; is_poison(TransientError|VendorError|ValueError)=False -> unknown treated as transient, fail-safe). worker/errors.py. RECONCILED with R2.0 (DECISIONS): the consistently-failing manuscript is retryable VendorError (ladder-then-DLQ per SPEC §1), NOT PoisonError; PoisonError reserved for structurally unprocessable messages. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 53 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 90 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Ack-last handler skeleton (BOM 08-W2 ⭐): worker/handlers/_base.py ack_last wrapper enforces the system's most important ordering (SPEC §4): do work -> COMMIT Postgres -> PUBLISH next -> ACK message (ack dead last). On TransientError -> route_retry_or_dlq THEN ack the original (work carried by the copy); on PoisonError -> publish to q.dlq THEN ack; on unknown -> treat as transient. Uses async with message.process(ignore_processed=True) so the explicit ack/nack doesn't double-process.
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k 'ack_last or base_handler' (success->ack; transient->ladder-then-ack; poison->dlq-then-ack; ack is last on every path, with a fake message)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k base_handler -> 7 passed (success->['ack']; TransientError->['route','ack'] max_retries=MAX_RETRIES; PoisonError->['route','ack'] max_retries=0 immediate DLQ; unknown ValueError->transient; parametrized ack-is-always-last). ack_last(ctx,live_queue,do_work) in worker/handlers/_base.py: async with message.process(ignore_processed=True) + explicit terminal ack; poison reuses route_retry_or_dlq with max_retries=0 (no broker change). Crash-mid-handler L4 is Phase-6 R3.1/R3.3; here L2. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 54 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 97 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Parse handler / fan-out emitter (BOM 08-W3 ⭐): consume JobCreated, load manuscript from MinIO, sim_parse (transient/poison), split_blocks (D3); if 0 blocks -> CAS job straight to STITCHING (+publish StitchReady) and return (fan-in of 0 must not hang); else in ONE tx INSERT N Task rows (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on job_id+block_index) and begin_parse CAS sets pending_count=N only on first run (H15); ALWAYS re-publish all N TtsRequested (redelivery that finds rows still emits events, H2); advance PARSING->GENERATING; ack last. Cap block count at MAX_BLOCKS (H14). NEVER inbox-skip parse.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'parse_fanout or zero_block_terminates' (N blocks -> N tasks + pending_count=N + N TtsRequested; redelivered JobCreated -> still N tasks, counter unchanged, events re-published; 0-block -> STITCHING/terminal)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k parse -> 5 passed (test_parse_fanout: 3 blocks -> 3 tasks + pending_count=3 + status GENERATING + 3 TtsRequested whose task_ids match the rows; test_parse_redelivery_is_idempotent: 2nd delivery -> still 2 tasks, counter still 2, events re-published; test_zero_block_terminates: whitespace manuscript -> 0 tasks, pending_count=0, status GENERATING, 1 StitchReady, 0 TtsRequested; test_parse_caps_blocks_at_max: MAX_BLOCKS=2 caps 4 blocks -> 2) against real pg+rmq+redis+minio. handle_parse in worker/handlers/parse.py: ON CONFLICT task inserts + begin_parse (H15, one tx) -> advance_status GENERATING -> ALWAYS re-publish (H2); deterministic task_id=job_id-i; 0-block emits StitchReady (FSM-legal realisation of 'straight to STITCHING'). advance_status CAS helper added to core/infra/queries.py. Live crash/poison-DLQ is Phase-6 R3.x; here L3. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 56 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 97 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: TTS handler / cache->slot->generate->fan-in (BOM 08-W4 ⭐): for each TtsRequested compute content_hash(text); cache_get BEFORE acquiring a semaphore slot (a hit burns NO token, SPEC §4); on miss acquire leased Redis slot (R2/X5, never asyncio.Semaphore) -> tts_fake_audio -> put_bytes tts/<hash>.wav -> cache_set -> release; then complete_task_and_decrement (B4 atomic claim+decrement); if remaining==0 publish StitchReady. H-EMIT: on redelivery where the claim no-ops, re-read pending_count and re-emit StitchReady if 0 (covers crash-after-decrement-before-publish). Ack last.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k 'tts_cache or tts_fan_in or tts_emit' (identical block -> one synth + no 2nd slot; N tasks -> exactly one StitchReady; redelivery after barrier crossed -> re-emits StitchReady)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k test_tts -> 3 passed (tts_fan_in: 3 tasks -> exactly one StitchReady, all DONE w/ audio_key, objects in MinIO; tts_cache: two identical blocks -> tts_fake_audio called exactly once, 2nd is cache hit; tts_emit/H-EMIT: redeliver already-DONE task after barrier crossed -> re-emits StitchReady) against real pg+rmq+redis+minio. handle_tts in worker/handlers/tts.py: reconstruct block text via block_index -> content_hash -> cache_get BEFORE semaphore slot (hit burns no token) -> H8 in-flight lock without slot -> leased slot synth -> put_bytes -> cache_set -> complete_task_and_decrement (B4) -> StitchReady on 0; H-EMIT re-reads pending_count on None. Live cache/fan_in/crash e2e is Phase-6 R4.2/R3.x; here L3. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 58 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 97 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: DLQ -> fan-in resolver (BOM 08-W7, H4): a consumer on q.dlq resolves the fan-in barrier for a poisoned TTS task so the job never stalls forever in GENERATING. Policy (recommended, documented in DOC1): mark that task FAILED and still decrement the barrier (stitch later skips FAILED blocks) so the job can reach STITCHING; if the decrement reaches 0, emit StitchReady. Stays OFF the hot queue (consumes q.dlq) so healthy q.tts traffic is unaffected (no HOL). Never silently drop a DLQ message without touching the barrier.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k dlq_resolver (poisoned TTS task on q.dlq -> task FAILED + barrier decremented; reaching 0 emits StitchReady; healthy traffic untouched)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k dlq_resolver -> 3 passed (tts poison: 1 good done + poison DLQ'd -> task FAILED, pending_count 0, one StitchReady (partial drama); parse poison JobCreated body -> job CAS FAILED; duplicate DLQ delivery -> no-op, pending_count stays 0 not -1, only one StitchReady) against real pg+rmq+redis+minio. RECOMMENDED policy implemented (FAILED-block + barrier resolves; alternative hard-fail is a one-line swap). handle_dlq routes by body shape (task_id -> fail_task_and_decrement; else advance_status FAILED); make_dlq_handler manages own ack/nack (not ack_last, q.dlq has no retry ladder), wired into run() OFF the hot queue. fail_task_and_decrement added to queries.py (guards NOT IN DONE,FAILED). X2 dispatch unchanged (1 passed). Live poison_pill e2e is Phase-6 R3.3; here L3. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 60 files 0 errors; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Stitch handler / idempotent finalize (BOM 08-W5): consume StitchReady, CAS job->STITCHING (skip if past), list the job's tts chunks in block order (skip FAILED-block keys per W7), CLIENT-SIDE concat (get_bytes each + join + put_bytes out/<job>.mp3 — NOT compose_object, which needs >=5MiB parts), CAS job->COMPLETED, ack last. Idempotent: a redelivered StitchReady when already COMPLETED -> ack and return (no double asset, no illegal COMPLETED->COMPLETED, H5). Status via CAS (rowcount-0 = someone else did it).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k stitch (happy path -> COMPLETED + out/<job>.mp3 exists; redelivered StitchReady -> still one asset, one COMPLETED)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k stitch -> 3 passed (test_stitch_happy_path: 3-block job -> COMPLETED, final_key=out/<job>.mp3, object == client-side concat of chunks in block order; test_stitch_redelivery_is_idempotent: 2nd StitchReady short-circuits on COMPLETED -> one asset, one COMPLETED; + the tts test matching 'stitch') against real pg+rmq+redis+minio. handle_stitch in worker/handlers/stitch.py: chunk order from DB (Task WHERE DONE ORDER BY block_index, FAILED skipped) not MinIO prefix (content-addressed); client-side concat (not compose_object, <5MiB parts); advance_status STITCHING then finalize_job CAS STITCHING->COMPLETED+final_key; H5 short-circuit if already COMPLETED. finalize_job added to queries.py. Live e2e is Phase-6 R4.3; here L3. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 61 files 0 errors; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Callback URL guard (BOM backlog-H-SSRF): worker/ssrf.py is_allowed(url, allowlist) defends the client-supplied callback_url against SSRF: parse host; if allowlist non-empty the host must be in it; resolve ALL A/AAAA records (socket.getaddrinfo) and reject if ANY resolved IP is private/loopback/link-local/reserved/multicast or 0.0.0.0/::. Never trust the hostname string alone (DNS rebinding). Pairs with follow_redirects=False + a hard timeout (W5b).
verification: uv run pytest tests/unit -k ssrf (169.254.169.254 blocked, localhost blocked, 10.x private blocked, allowlisted public host allowed, non-allowlisted public host blocked when allowlist set)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/unit -k ssrf -> 9 passed (metadata 169.254.169.254 blocked, localhost/127.0.0.1 blocked, 10.x private blocked, 0.0.0.0 unspecified blocked, allowlisted public host allowed, non-allowlisted public blocked when allowlist set, DNS-rebinding multi-record with one private blocked, unresolvable host blocked, host-less url blocked) with socket.getaddrinfo mocked (no network). is_allowed in worker/ssrf.py: allowlist check + resolve ALL records + reject private/loopback/link-local/reserved/multicast/unspecified. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 63 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 106 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Webhook notify / failure != job failure (BOM 08-W5, MUST #8): after COMPLETED, if callback_url is set and passes the H-SSRF guard, POST a small JSON payload (job_id, status, final_key) with httpx (follow_redirects=False, hard timeout). Wrapped in try/except: a webhook failure is logged and swallowed — the job stays COMPLETED. Never retry the webhook on the pipeline retry ladder (would ride a completed job into the DLQ).
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k webhook (unreachable/failing callback -> job still COMPLETED with asset, failure logged; not-allowlisted URL -> skipped/log-only)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k webhook -> 3 passed (delivered: allowlisted+allowed URL receives JSON payload {job_id,status=COMPLETED,final_key}; failure-tolerant: httpx.ConnectError swallowed -> job stays COMPLETED with asset intact (MUST #8); log-only: empty allowlist -> httpx.AsyncClient never constructed) against real pg+rmq+redis+minio. _notify in worker/handlers/stitch.py called after finalize (outside raise-path, never on retry ladder): no callback -> skip; empty allowlist -> log-only; else is_allowed (H-SSRF) guard then httpx.post(follow_redirects=False, timeout=WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_S), all failures logged+swallowed. Live e2e is Phase-6 R4.3; here L3. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 64 files 0 errors, pytest tests/unit 106 passed; commit f62825c HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Manuscript max-size guard at ingestion (BOM backlog-H13): an unbounded manuscript body buffered in gateway memory is a DoS vector. Enforce MAX_MANUSCRIPT_BYTES (F0.2): reject oversized request bodies with a clean machine-readable 413 BEFORE the body is buffered into the pydantic model (a Content-Length pre-check in HTTP middleware, since checking len(body.manuscript) in the route is already too late to prevent the OOM). Preserve the load-bearing dual-write order (MinIO -> DB commit -> publish) for the accepted path.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k ingestion (a body over the cap -> 413 structured JSON; a normal body -> 202 as before)
features.evidence: uv run pytest tests/integration -k ingestion -> 9 passed (7 original + 2 new H13: a >1MB body -> 413 {error:'manuscript_too_large', max_bytes:1000000}; a normal body -> 202 unchanged) against real pg+rmq+minio via the gateway lifespan. guard_manuscript_size HTTP middleware in gateway/main.py inspects Content-Length BEFORE the body is buffered into CreateJobRequest (the route-level len() check would be too late to prevent OOM, H13 MUST); rejects with machine-readable 413 JSON (R2.2c contract). Bounds the realistic honest-Content-Length vector; chunked-no-Content-Length residual documented (the 'stream to MinIO' alternative). Dual-write order untouched on the accepted path. full gate: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 65 files 0 errors; commit e48428e HEAD anchor (user handles git)

Same L3 verify as W3 (precedent: G7->R5.1, G8->R3.4 flipped directly). make check green (106 unit, mypy 67 files). commit 700e587 HEAD anchor (user handles git).

features.behavior: E2E harness & fixtures (BOM 11-T4): tests/e2e/conftest.py + helpers.py provide the shared L4 scaffolding every probe reuses — a `stack` fixture that ensures the 6-service compose stack is healthy AND running CURRENT code (rebuilds the app images; the pre-Phase-4 image is an idle skeleton that consumes nothing), an httpx client to http://localhost:8000, wait_for_status(job_id, target, timeout) poller, publish_raw(event, queue) duplicate-injector (localhost broker), kill_container/restart docker helpers, and a poison_manuscript() builder. Everything tagged @pytest.mark.e2e so the no-Docker make check stays green.
verification: uv run pytest -m e2e -k harness_smoke (POST a trivial job -> reaches COMPLETED through the live stack)
features.evidence: uv run pytest -m e2e -k harness_smoke -> 1 passed in 104s: a real 2-block manuscript POSTed to the LIVE compose gateway travels gateway->q.parse->worker(parse->tts->stitch)->COMPLETED, observed via GET /status polling. tests/e2e/conftest.py (stack: docker compose up -d --build + gateway /health poll, ensures CURRENT code since the pre-Phase-4 image is an idle skeleton; client; wait_for_status poller) + helpers.py (kill/stop/start/restart_container, poison_manuscript via POISON_MARKER). L4 surfaced + fixed TWO real deploy bugs L3 could not: (1) httpx absent from services/worker runtime deps -> worker crash-looped on import (added httpx>=0.27, re-locked); (2) NO schema in compose Postgres -> POST /jobs 500 'relation jobs does not exist' (init.sh never migrated) -> added idempotent create_tables to the gateway lifespan (single schema owner). make check green: ruff+fmt clean, mypy 70 files 0 errors, 106 unit passed. See DECISIONS 2026-06-25 'Phase 6 / T1'. commit 700e587 base (fixes uncommitted; user handles git).

features.behavior: Crash recovery: manual ack LAST (after Postgres commit), prefetch set, NACK->retry path. docker kill mid-job loses no message.
verification: make e2e -k crash_recovery (docker kill worker mid-job -> redelivered, job converges)
features.evidence: uv run pytest -m e2e -k crash_recovery -> 1 passed in 42.78s (test_job_survives_worker_crash_and_completes_on_recovery) against the LIVE compose stack. DETERMINISTIC design: the sim's only vendor latency is asyncio.sleep(0) so a job processes in ms — a mid-handler kill cannot be timed without racing. Instead: docker kill WORKER first, POST a 5-block job, assert it stays PENDING across a 5s outage (JobCreated sits durably in q.parse, no consumer, no loss), docker start WORKER, then assert COMPLETED + final_key set + pending_count==0 within 300s (worker re-runs its uv-sync entrypoint ~30s, reconnects, runs full parse->tts->stitch). A lost message would strand the job in PENDING forever, so reaching COMPLETED IS the no-loss proof. The complementary in-flight unacked-redelivery path (ack-last) is L3-proven by the idempotency handlers (B4 conditional claim, W4 H-EMIT, W5 stitch H5); split documented in DECISIONS Phase-6 R3.1. commit 700e587 base (uncommitted; user handles git).

features.behavior: Idempotent consumers: processed_events inbox (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) + task:done SETNX. Duplicate delivery has exactly-once effect; no double pending_count decrement.
verification: make e2e -k duplicate_delivery (same JobCreated x2 -> single effect, counter correct)

features.behavior: DLQ: poison pill dead-letters after 3 retries with exponential backoff, off the hot queue (no head-of-line blocking of healthy traffic). Job -> FAILED.
verification: make e2e -k poison_pill (-> DLQ after 3 attempts; concurrent healthy jobs still complete)

features.behavior: PENDING-sweeper/reconciler (G8, closes the gateway dual-write seam BACKLOG H1): periodic task re-publishes JobCreated for jobs stuck PENDING past a generous timeout. The Job row IS its own outbox; idempotent parse (R3.2) makes a re-publish safe. 'Ack last' is a consumer rule and cannot cover the gateway producer.
verification: uv run pytest tests/integration -k sweeper (insert Job(PENDING) with NO event published -> after one sweep JobCreated is on q.parse and the job progresses)
features.evidence: G8 done. uv run pytest tests/integration/test_sweeper.py -> 4 passed (orphaned PENDING with NO event published -> sweep_once re-publishes JobCreated to q.parse, count>=1; swept job stays PENDING = MUST NOT mutate status; fresh PENDING within generous timeout left untouched count==0; GENERATING job never re-driven) against real postgres:17-alpine + rabbitmq:4. gateway/sweeper.py: sweep_once(engine,exchange,pending_timeout_s) selects only PENDING job_ids older than DB-side now()-timeout (no Python row-scan, no status write) and re-publishes JobCreated; safe ONLY because parse is idempotent/re-runnable (H2 ON CONFLICT + H15 begin_parse CAS). run_sweeper loops it every SWEEP_INTERVAL_S (sleep-first so it never fires on boot / in fast tests), launched as an asyncio.Task in the gateway lifespan and cancelled cleanly on shutdown. Regression: full gateway trio (ingestion+stats+sweeper) 16 passed with the sweeper live in the lifespan. H10 retention scheduling left as a separate optional chore (not folded in, to keep every added line tested). Live crash-seam e2e is Phase-6; here L3. full gate: make check 106 unit green, ruff+fmt clean, mypy 67 files 0 errors; commit e48428e HEAD anchor (user handles git)

features.behavior: Global TTS semaphore in Redis (leased token list, TTL): max 3 concurrent across ALL workers; a crashed worker's slot auto-reclaims via TTL.
verification: make e2e -k semaphore (>3 requested -> never >3 in flight; killed holder's slot returns)

features.behavior: TTS handler: content-hash cache check BEFORE acquiring a slot (cache hit burns no token); PUT tts/<hash>.wav; SETEX cache. Fan-in via atomic UPDATE...RETURNING -> exactly one worker sees 0 and emits StitchReady.
verification: make e2e -k cache (identical block -> no 2nd vendor call) AND -k fan_in (exactly one StitchReady)

features.behavior: Stitch & notify: concat chunks -> out/<job>.mp3 in MinIO, Job COMPLETED, fire webhook/log. Webhook failure does NOT fail the job.
verification: make e2e -k stitch (full happy path -> COMPLETED + asset) AND -k webhook_failure_still_completed
